---
metadata-files: 
  - metadata.yml
format: 
    revealjs:
      pagetitle: "Statistical MDS"
      # logo: imgs/extra/ucsd-small.jpeg
      footer: "[Job Talk • 2026]{.smaller}"
      slide-number: true
      width: 1760
      height: 990
      theme:
        - src/css/theme.scss
      include-in-header:
        - file: src/header.html
        - src/chalkboard.html
      css: src/css/imgbox.css
      filters:
        - src/scale-image.lua
      controls: true
      touch: true
      scrollable: true
      history: false
      navigation-mode: linear
      code-tools: true
      chalkboard:
        theme: whiteboard
        boardmarker-width	: 2
      # embed-resources: true
      self-contained-math: true
jupyter: job-talk
---



## {.center}

Job Talk • 2026
+br+br+br+br+br

<p></p>

### [A Statistical Framework for+br Multidimensional Scaling From Noisy Data]{.Large}

<o></p>
[Siddharth Vishwanath]{.ucsd-gold .Large}
<o></p>
![](imgs/extra/ucsd.png){width=16%}

+br+br+br+br

::: footer
:::

# Motivation

::: {.large .center}
+br+br
Modern data is not only big, but also structurally complex. +br+br
Science, engineering, technology, and society increasingly rely+br on understanding these complex patterns in data.
:::

{{< include src/math/tex-macros >}}
$$
\require{enclose}
\require{physics}
\require{ams}
$$




## Under the hood: Fuk-Nagaev-Type concentration {#appendix-fuk-nagaev visibility="uncounted"}



Let $\Eps = (\eps_{ij})$ be an $n \times n$ symmetric random matrix with symmetric entries.

:::: {.fragment fragment-index=1 .semi-fade-out}
:::{.content-box-dblue .small}
**Bounded Concentration [@bandeira2016sharp].** &emsp; Suppose [$\max_{i,j}|{\eps_{i,j}|} \le K$ a.s.]{.magenta}. +br Then, with probability greater than $1 - O({e^{-t}})$,
$$
\norm{\Eps}_2 \lesssim {\max_{i}\sqrt{\sum_{j} \Var(\eps_{ij})} + K \sqrt{t + \log{n}}}
$$
:::
::::

+br

:::: {.fragment fragment-index=1 .fade-in}
:::{.content-box-orange .small}
**Fuk-Nagaev-Type Concentration [@vishwanath2025minimax]** &emsp; Suppose [$\max_{i,j}\|{\eps_{i,j}\|_{L^q}} \le \sigma$ for $q > 4$]{.magenta}+br Then, for all $0 < r < \frac{(q-4)}{2}$, with probability greater than $1 - O({e^{-t}} + {n^{-r}})$,
$$
\norm{\Eps}_2 \lesssim {\max_{i}\sqrt{\sum_{j} \Var(\eps_{ij})} + \sigma n^{(r+2)/q} \sqrt{t + \log{n}}}
$$
:::
::::



## Illustration

::: {.center .r-stack}
![](imgs/mds/plts/p2.png){width=55% .fragment .fade-out fragment-index=1}

![](imgs/mds/plts/p1.png){width=98% .fragment .fade-in fragment-index=1}
:::

::: {.footnote .scriptsize}
more [experiments in the appendix](#appendix-fourth-moment-plots)
:::








## 
::: {#refs}
:::
